description: Phase 5 training fueling engine for mealplan: implement deterministic training_carbs_g rules from normalized zone minutes as a pure domain service.

userStories.title: Implement pure domain fueling calculator
userStories.description: As an application orchestrator, I want a pure domain function for training fueling so later orchestration can call one deterministic entrypoint.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `calculate_training_carbs_g(zones_minutes: Mapping[int, int]) -> float` in `src/mealplan/domain/services.py`.
Input contract is already-normalized `dict[int, int]` with canonical keys 1..5 only (no raw CLI payload shape).
Re-export `calculate_training_carbs_g` from `src/mealplan/domain/__init__.py` for stable cross-layer imports.
Function returns training_carbs_g as float.
Function has no side effects and no dependency on CLI/application modules.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Implement all-zone-1 fueling rule
userStories.description: As a nutrition calculation engine, I want training that occurs only in zone 1 to produce zero fueling carbs so low-intensity sessions are handled per requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: If all non-zero minutes are in zone 1, result is exactly 0.0.
If total minutes are 0, result is exactly 0.0.
Unit tests assert strict float equality for both cases.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Implement zone-2-plus override rule
userStories.description: As a nutrition calculation engine, I want any zone 2-5 minutes to trigger fueling based on total duration so moderate/high intensity sessions are fueled consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: If any zone 2..5 has minutes > 0, result uses sum(all zone minutes) * 1.0.
Zone 1 minutes are included in total duration once override is triggered.
Unit tests verify representative zone 2, 3, 4, and 5 presence scenarios.
Unit tests assert strict float outputs such as 30.0, 60.0, and 95.0.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Enforce deterministic precedence behavior
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want explicit precedence tests so regressions cannot invert the all-Z1 versus any-Z2+ behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests include mixed sessions with zone 1 plus zone 2..5 minutes and confirm override path.
Tests include boundary sessions with only zone 1 minutes and confirm zero path.
Tests document precedence intent in case names.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Keep validation boundary from Phase 3
userStories.description: As an architect, I want fueling logic to trust Phase 3 normalization/validation so boundaries remain clean and rules are not duplicated.
acceptanceCriteria: Phase 5 service assumes normalized zone keys and values from Phase 3.
No duplicate validation for out-of-range zone keys or negative minutes is added in Phase 5.
Tests use normalized valid zone payloads only.
Documentation note targets are explicit by topic: `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` (technical topics), `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md` (functional topics), and `docs/MODEL.md` (domain model topics).
Document that malformed input rejection remains a Phase 3 concern, while Phase 5 consumes normalized inputs.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Add medium-depth permutation test matrix
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a representative matrix of single-zone and mixed-zone cases so fueling rule behavior is proven without full combinatorial explosion.
acceptanceCriteria: Add parameterized tests for only zone 1 active, only one zone in 2..5 active, mixed zone 1 plus zone 2..5, multiple zones in 2..5, and all zeros.
Test matrix size is medium-depth and representative, not exhaustive combinations.
Expected values are explicit strict floats in each case.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Expose stable interface for later orchestration
userStories.description: As a Phase 8 implementer, I want a stable domain API contract for fueling so orchestration can integrate without refactoring this phase's core logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Canonical orchestration-facing API is `calculate_training_carbs_g(zones_minutes: Mapping[int, int]) -> float` in `src/mealplan/domain/services.py`.
The API accepts normalized zone minutes only and does not accept request DTOs or CLI payloads.
Return semantics are documented as always float and deterministic for the same input.
No Phase 5 application wrapper is introduced.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.
